semantic.role: Defines the pattern-matching rules for parsing TypeScript/JavaScript imports, exports, and usages within the ArchSpine code analysis engine.
responsibilities: Specifying import pattern definitions (named, default, namespace)
Specifying export pattern definitions (function, class, interface, type, const, let, var, default, list)
Specifying usage pattern definitions (call, new, property)
Mapping matched syntax nodes to semantic kinds (e.g., Function, Class, Interface, Type, Variable, Unknown)
outOfScope: AST traversal logic beyond pattern matching
Dependency resolution logic (e.g., module paths, cyclic detection)

English.purpose: This document provides the pattern definitions that ArchSpine uses to identify and categorize code structures such as imports, exports, and usage references in TypeScript/JavaScript files. It acts as the configuration for a structural code parser, enabling the system to understand how modules relate to one another.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers maintaining the ArchSpine analysis engine, particularly those responsible for extending or refining the set of recognized patterns. It assumes familiarity with tree-sitter grammar syntax and TypeScript/JavaScript module systems.
key_takeaways: Imports are captured via three pattern variants: named, default, and namespace.
Exports encompass a wide range of declarations (functions, classes, interfaces, types, variables) and are tagged with a 'kind' for downstream processing.
Usage patterns cover function calls, constructor invocations, and property accesses to track reference relationships.
The document is exclusively concerned with syntactic pattern matching, not semantic analysis or dependency resolution.
